status: MACHINE-PREPARED — NOT ACTED ON. No card is removed or altered by this file.
semantics: Cards whose transformation is bodily rather than facial — prosthetic limbs, stature, physique — with the performer own face visible on screen. They are graded honestly by the face rule (docs/TRANSFORM-RUBRIC.md standing ruling 2). Whether such a card belongs on a wall about performers who vanish under a designed FACE is an eligibility question and the owner decision; removal is never automated.

rows.basis: Ruling 2: sword-legs are a bodily prosthetic, not facial. No facial makeup/prosthetics described. Boutella's face is fully visible on screen, so grade honestly by the face rule (1) rather than inflating for body transformation.

Facial change is essentially a beard (limited appliance); the twisted spine and neck scar cited in the reveal are bodily/neck, not facial, per standing ruling 2 — flagging since filed grade appears to credit bodily elements toward a facial score.
Lurch's height is bodily, not facial; on-screen his own face is visible under light pale/gaunt makeup — surface transformation, recognizable at a glance. Eligibility flag set per ruling 2.

Prosthetic teeth and bleached brows are limited/partial appliances, not full-face paint or redesign; weight gain is bodily. Fits grade 2 (limited makeup, dental pieces) — recognizable at a glance despite the Oscar-winning transformation.

Fingleton was cast purely for natural extreme stature; no makeup, prosthetics, or facial design applied. Ruling 2: bodily/stature-based casting with a fully visible face grades honestly by the face rule (1), not inflated for scale.
